name: one-time-latency-blip
description: One trace took 25s due to external web-search timeout. All other 49 traces normal (p95: 6.2s). One-time blip.

item.title: Latency anomaly detected on customer-support-bot
item.description: Automated alert: trace latency exceeded 20s threshold on customer-support-bot.
item.note:
Investigated the latency alert. Findings:

- Single trace (trace-id: lat-8842) took 25.3s at 11:14:22 UTC
- Root cause: the web-search tool call in span ws-0042 timed out after 20.1s before returning partial results
- The agent retried with cached results and completed successfully
- All other 49 traces in the same hour had normal latency: p50 2.8s, p95 6.2s, max (excluding outlier) 7.1s
- No other traces triggered the web-search tool in this period — this was the only one with external dependency
- Checked the web-search provider status page: they reported a brief connectivity issue at 11:14 UTC, resolved by 11:16 UTC
- No errors in the trace — it completed with status OK, just slow
- Historical check: no similar timeouts in the past 7 days

This appears to be a one-off external dependency timeout. No action needed.

projectMemory:
## Project Agents
- **customer-support-bot**: Handles customer inquiries via RAG. Typical trace has 3-5 LLM calls, 1-2 retrieval steps. Normal latency: 2-4s per trace.
- **email-classifier**: Classifies incoming emails. Single LLM call per trace, ~500ms.

## Baselines
- Error rate: <2%
- Daily cost: ~$5
- Trace volume: ~200/day
